title: Missing NULL check after GetStringUTFChars in frodoKemCreateContext
problem: The JNI function `GetStringUTFChars` can return NULL if the JVM runs out of memory. If it returns NULL, passing it to `strcmp` in `getFrodoKemParamId` will cause a segmentation fault and crash the JVM.

title: Missing NULL check after GetStringUTFChars in frodoKemGenerateKeyPair
problem: `GetStringUTFChars` can return NULL. Passing NULL to `getFrodoKemParamId` will result in a segmentation fault.

title: Missing NULL check after GetByteArrayElements for encapsulate key in frodoKemSetKeys
problem: `GetByteArrayElements` can return NULL on out-of-memory. Using this NULL pointer or passing it to `ReleaseByteArrayElements` will cause undefined behavior or crash the JVM.

title: Missing NULL check after GetByteArrayElements for decapsulate key in frodoKemSetKeys
problem: `GetByteArrayElements` can return NULL on out-of-memory. Using this NULL pointer or passing it to `ReleaseByteArrayElements` will cause undefined behavior or crash the JVM.

problem: `GetStringUTFChars` can return NULL. Passing NULL to `getMcElieceParamId` will result in a segmentation fault.

title: Missing NULL check after GetStringUTFChars in mcelieceGenerateKeyPair
problem: `GetStringUTFChars` can return NULL. Passing NULL to `getMcElieceParamId` will result in a segmentation fault.

title: Missing NULL check after GetByteArrayElements for encapsulate key in mcelieceSetKeys
problem: `GetByteArrayElements` can return NULL on out-of-memory. Using this NULL pointer or passing it to `ReleaseByteArrayElements` will cause undefined behavior or crash the JVM.

title: Missing NULL check after GetByteArrayElements for decapsulate key in mcelieceSetKeys
problem: `GetByteArrayElements` can return NULL on out-of-memory. Using this NULL pointer or passing it to `ReleaseByteArrayElements` will cause undefined behavior or crash the JVM.

title: Incorrect exception type thrown for short buffers
problem: When generating a secret into an existing array that is too small, the implementation throws `IllegalStateException`. According to the JCE contract for `KeyAgreementSpi.engineGenerateSecret(byte[], int)`, a `javax.crypto.ShortBufferException` must be thrown in this case to allow calling applications to handle the size dynamically.

title: Incorrect exception type thrown for short buffers
problem: When generating a secret into an existing array that is too small, the implementation throws `IllegalStateException`. According to the JCE contract for `KeyAgreementSpi.engineGenerateSecret(byte[], int)`, a `javax.crypto.ShortBufferException` must be thrown in this case to allow calling applications to handle the size dynamically.
